note:
该诉讼审视了Facebook整个公司历史上的行为，指控该公司“非法”并以“掠夺性”方式收购了竞争对手，以发展并保持其市场力量。该诉讼以Facebook收购Instagram和WhatsApp为例。
 州政府要求美国联邦哥伦比亚特区地方法院“禁止Facebook进一步进行价值不超过1000万美元的收购”，而无需事先通知原告各州。该诉讼还要求法院“其认为适当的任何其他救济措施，包括剥离或重组非法收购的公司或现有的Facebook资产或业务线。”
  诉讼由加州，科罗拉多州，佛罗里达州，爱荷华州，内布拉斯加州，北卡罗来纳州，俄亥俄州，田纳西州和哥伦比亚特区的总检察长委员会领导，纽约总检察长莱蒂蒂亚·詹姆斯（Letitia James）牵头。
 詹姆斯说：“美国几乎每个州都参加了两党诉讼，因为Facebook统治市场的努力既非法又有害。” “今天的诉讼应该向Facebook和其他所有公司发出明确的信息，即扼杀竞争，减少创新或削减隐私保护的任何努力都将在我们办公室的全面支持下实现。”
  美国联邦贸易委员会（FTC）投票通过了针对社交媒体巨头的反垄断诉讼，就在同一周，针对Facebook的州反托拉斯诉讼得以落实。投票结果使对Facebook业务进行了为期20个月的调查，其中包括对WhatsApp和Instagram的收购-这两个以前独立的应用已被纳入社交巨头的业务中。 FTC现在呼吁这些公司独立出来。
 Facebook经常因其影响力过大而受到批评，并多次被要求就相关问题在国会作证，但在联邦和州一级进行的反托拉斯协调工作标志着该公司面临新的挑战。
note_en:
The  lawsuit, which looks at Facebook’s actions throughout the company’s history, alleges that the company bought competitors “illegally” and in a “predatory manner” in order to grow and preserve its market power. The suit cites Facebook’s acquisitions of Instagram and WhatsApp as prominent examples.
 The collection of states asks the U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia to “restrain Facebook from making further acquisitions valued at or in excess of $10 million” without notifying the plaintiff states in advance. The lawsuit also asks the court for “any additional relief it determines is appropriate, including the divestiture or restructuring of illegally acquired companies, or current Facebook assets or business lines.”
  The suit was led by a committee of attorneys general from California, Colorado, Florida, Iowa, Nebraska, North Carolina, Ohio, Tennessee and the District of Columbia, with New York Attorney General Letitia James spearheading the effort.
 “Almost every state in this nation has joined this bipartisan lawsuit because Facebook’s efforts to dominate the market were as illegal as they were harmful,” James said. “Today’s suit should send a clear message to Facebook and every other company that any efforts to stifle competition, reduce innovation, or cut privacy protections will be met with the full force of our offices.”
  The state antitrust action against Facebook materialized the same week that the  FTC voted to pursue its own antitrust suit against the social media giant. That vote tied a bow on a  20-month investigation into Facebook’s business, including its acquisitions of WhatsApp and Instagram — two formerly independent apps that were folded into the social giant’s business. The FTC is now calling for those companies to be spun out independently.
 Facebook has been regularly criticized for its outsized influence and repeatedly called to testify before Congress on related issues, but the coordinated antitrust effort at the federal and state level marks a challenging new chapter for the company.
title: Facebook遭到来自46个州的大规模反托拉斯诉讼
title_en: Facebook hit with massive antitrust lawsuit from 46 states

note:
监管机构说，有重大过敏反应史的人不应服用辉瑞/ BioNTech Covid刺戳。  该建议适用于对药物，食品或疫苗有反应的人。  两人在接受新刺刺后不久就做出了反应，接受了治疗，现在都很好。  据了解，他们患有类过敏反应，往往会引起皮疹，呼吸困难，有时还会导致血压下降。 这与可能导致致命的过敏反应不同。  两位NHS工人都有严重的过敏史，并随身携带肾上腺素笔。  英国国家医疗服务体系（NHS）医疗主任斯蒂芬·波维斯（Stephen Powis）教授说，两个人的康复情况都很好。  MHRA负责人June Raine博士说，只有有了我们的经验，才有必要采取这一步骤。 
像这样的反应并不常见，但是其他疫苗（包括每年的流感疫苗）也会发生这种反应。  在英国推出新的Covid戳戳的第一天，数千人在医院诊所接受了疫苗接种。  伦敦帝国理工学院免疫学专家Peter Openshaw教授说：＆＃34;我们很快就了解了这两种过敏反应，并且监管机构已对此采取行动以发出预防性建议，这一事实表明该监控系统正在运行 好吧。  没有有效的药物就没有副作用，因此您必须权衡风险和收益。  请记住，今年英国有千分之一的人死于冠状病毒感染后死亡，而且这一数字每天都在上升。  在昨天接种的数千种疫苗中，有两个人有过敏反应，他们从中恢复了过来。  任何疫苗都可能发生这种反应，并用类固醇或肾上腺素等药物治疗。 
试验报告每千人接种一次可能与变种有关的过敏反应。  MHRA已向面临风险最大的人群提供有针对性的建议，但是对于绝大多数人而言，这并没有改变。  NHS宣布疫苗接种计划将从下周开始扩展到GP手术之后。  最初，预计将向200例GP手术提供剂量，以便于周二开始使用。 80岁以上的人将被首先邀请。  一旦首批200名GP医师接受了剂量，该计划将扩展到1,000多个外科手术-每个局部地区都有指定的地点。  这意味着大多数患者将被邀请去一个普通的GP中心。  英国医学会全科医生负责人Richard Vautrey博士说，全科医生已准备就绪。 
＆＃34;我们在提供疫苗方面拥有丰富的经验-每周能够做数百万人。它实际上取决于供应以及我们能多快获得它。＆＃34;
  同时，由牛津大学和阿斯利康公司开发的另一种Covid疫苗背后的关键人物之一淡化了对刺戳可能对老年人无效的担忧。
 《柳叶刀》周二发布的数据表明，在55岁以上的人群中疫苗的有效性尚缺乏确定性。
 莎拉·吉尔伯特（Sarah Gilbert）教授说，老年人只是在此过程的后期被招募参加试验，而下一次试验的结果很可能包括有关刺戳在55岁以上人群中表现良好的信息。
 但是她说没有什么区别。在较早的试验中，在年轻人和70岁以上的人群中发现了免疫应答。
 她说，这意味着监管机构可以支持将疫苗发放给老年人。
  该疫苗对于快速推出至关重要，因为它无需保存在超冷温度下，因此更易于存储和分发。该国目前有超过500万剂疫苗。 
英国周三发布的最新每日数据显示，在Covid-19阳性试验的28天内，现在又有533人死亡。 另有16,578人对Covid-19进行了阳性检测，使英国的总病例达到1,766,819。
note_en:
People with a history of significant allergic reactions should not have the Pfizer/BioNTech Covid jab, regulators say.
  The advice applies to those who have had reactions to medicines, food or vaccines, the Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency said.
 The two people had a reaction shortly after having the new jab, had treatment and are both fine now.
 They are understood to have had an anaphylactoid reaction, which tends to involve a skin rash, breathlessness and sometimes a drop in blood pressure. This is not the same as anaphylaxis which can be fatal.
 Both NHS workers have a history of serious allergies and carry adrenaline pens around with them.
  Professor Stephen Powis, medical director for the NHS in England, said both individuals were recovering well.
  Dr June Raine, head of the MHRA, said it was only right to take this step now that "we've had this experience".
 Reactions like this are uncommon, but do happen with other vaccines, including the annual flu jab.
 Several thousand people were vaccinated on Tuesday in hospital clinics on the first day of the UK rollout of the new Covid jab.
 Prof Peter Openshaw, an expert in immunology at Imperial College London, said: "The fact that we know so soon about these two allergic reactions and that the regulator has acted on this to issue precautionary advice shows that this monitoring system is working well."
   No effective medicine is without side effects so you have to balance the risk and the benefit.
 Remember, one in a thousand people in the UK have died after being infected with coronavirus this year and that figure is rising daily.
 Two people, out of thousands vaccinated yesterday, had an allergic reaction which they recovered from.
 Such reactions can happen with any vaccine and are treated with drugs such as steroids or adrenaline.
 The trials reported  one possible allergic reaction per thousand people immunised that may have been related to the jab.
 The MHRA has given targeted advice to those most at risk, but for the overwhelming majority of people, this changes nothing.
    The development came after the NHS has announced the vaccination programme will be expanded out to GP surgeries from next week.
 Doses are expected to be delivered to around 200 GP surgeries initially to allow them to start on Tuesday. The over-80s will be invited first.
 Once the first 200 GP practices have received their doses the programme will be expanded out to more than 1,000 surgeries - with each local area having a designated site.
 It means most patients will be invited to a GP centre that is not their usual one.
   Dr Richard Vautrey, GP leader from the British Medical Association, said GPs were "ready to go".
 "We have a wealth of experience in delivering vaccines - and will be able to do millions of people a week. It is really dependent on supply and how quickly we can get our hands on it."
  Meanwhile, one of the key people behind another Covid vaccine developed by Oxford University and AstraZeneca has played down fears the jab may not be effective in older people.
 Data published by the Lancet on Tuesday suggested there was a lack of certainty over the effectiveness of the vaccine in the over-55s.
 Prof Sarah Gilbert said older people were only recruited into the trials later in the process, and the next trial results provided were likely to include information about how well the jab worked in people over the age of 55.
 But she said there "was no difference" in the immune response seen in younger adults and people over 70 in earlier trials.
 This meant the regulator could support licensing of the vaccine to the elderly, she said.
  The vaccine is crucial to fast rollout as it is much easier to store and distribute, because it does not need to be kept at ultra-cold temperatures. There are more than 5 million doses of the vaccine currently in the country.
 The latest  daily figures for the UK, published on Wednesday, showed a further 533 people have now died within 28 days of a positive Covid-19 test. A further 16,578 people have tested positive for Covid-19, taking the UK's total cases to 1,766,819.
title: Covid-19疫苗：过敏警告

生物技术显然正在迅速发展，到2020年，将有成千上万的人有能力造成灾难性的生物灾难。我关心的不仅是有组织的恐怖组织，而且还有那些现在正在设计计算机病毒的人的思维方式下的个人怪兽。即使所有国家都对潜在危险技术强加了有效的法规，但在我看来，积极执法的力度与毒品法的情况一样小。所谓“生物错误”，我的意思是与恐怖袭击具有相同的作用，但源于无心而不是邪恶的意图。
  1.认知偏见加剧了灾难的主观可能性：如果可以想象到一种情况，那似乎是有可能的。 2.道德市场力量也扭曲了可能性：悲观主义者被认为是认真负责的，乐观主义者被视为自满和天真。由于1＆amp; 2，大多数世界末日的预测都是错误的（美苏之间的核战争，Y2K灾难，人口炸弹，资源战争，每周9/11规模的恐怖袭击等）。 3.贝叶斯基准利率：如果从未发生过极端的特定事件，则赔率相对较高。过去唯一成功的生物恐怖案例是Rajneeshee邪教（751例，0例死亡）和炭疽邮件（5例）。苏维埃时代的炭疽生物武器泄漏又造成66人死亡。4.潜在肇事者：渴望无​​意义破坏的人数很少（否则，每个城市每天都有轰炸，大规模枪击，破坏活动等），而不是零星的头条新闻）。将分数乘以知识，技能，才智和纪律，以制造出超级细菌（与笨拙的内衣轰炸机和Tsarnaev Brother类型相反）。池子可能还会更小：我怀疑非常聪明会降低发生大规模死亡和混乱事件的几率。 5.墨菲定律：乘以逃避检测和事故的比例（例如，被自己的生物武器打击），尽管其流行病学过程不可预测，但其计划仍然成功。 6.进一步乘以黑帽击败白帽（即世界医学和流行病学网络）的机会，后者将利用收容措施，疫苗，抗生素等与疾病爆发作斗争。乘积不是0，而是我不认为这很高。当然，我有15年的后见之明：我只需要再3个无疫情的年份。但是马丁爵士受益于合成生物学不可预见的进步，例如CRISPR / Cas9。我们需要“伤亡”的定义（死亡，永久性残疾还是仅仅是感染？），＆＃34;生物恐怖症＆＃34; （由国家发起？政治复仇/狂杀？）和＆＃34; bioerror＆＃34; （我认为仅仅缺乏准备就不算在内）。但这不应该太难。
    死亡人数将由WHO，CDC或BPHS定义，以人数最高者为准。理想的伤亡应包括“需要住院的受害者”。并且不包括由病原体引起的间接死亡，尽管Long Now最终将依靠上述组织设定的标准来确定伤亡人数来裁定该赌注。
note_en:
Biotechnology is plainly advancing rapidly, and by 2020 there will be thousands-even millions-of people with the capability to cause a catastrophic biological disaster. My concern is not only organized terrorist groups, but individual wierdos with the mindset of the people who now design computer viruses. Even if all nations impose effective regulations on potentially dangerous technologies, the cyhance of an active enforcement seems to me as small as in the case of the drug laws.  By "bioerror", I mean something which has the same effect as a terror attack, but rises from inadvertance rather than evil intent.
  1. Cognitive biases inflate the subjective odds of disaster: If a scenario is imaginable, it seems probable.  2. Moral market forces also distort the odds: Pessimists are seen as serious and responsible, optimists as complacent and naive. Because of 1 & 2, most doomsday predictions are false (thermonuclear war between the US and USSR, Y2K disaster, the population bomb, resource wars, weekly 9/11-scale terrorist attacks, etc.).  3. Bayesian base rate: If an extreme and specific category of event has never before happened, the odds are stacked against it. The only successful past cases of bioterror are the Rajneeshee cult (751 cases, 0 deaths) and the Anthrax mailing (5 deaths). The Soviet-era anthrax bioweapon leak killed another 66.  4. Pool of potential perpetrators: The number of people eager for senseless destruction is small (otherwise we'd have daily bombings, mass shootings, sabotage, etc. in every city, rather than sporadic headline-worthy ones). Multiply by the fraction with the knowledge, skill, intelligence, and discipline to engineer a superbug (as opposed to the bumbling underwear-bomber and Tsarnaev Brother types). The pool may be smaller still: I suspect that being very smart lowers the odds of enjoying mass death and mayhem.  5. Murphy's Law: Multiply further by the fraction who escape detection and accidents (e.g., being stricken by one's own bioweapon), and whose plan succeeds despite the unpredictability of epidemiological processes.  6. Multiply further by the chance that the Black Hat will defeat the White Hats, namely the world's medical and epidemiological networks, who would combat an outbreak with containment, vaccines, antibiotics, etc. The product is not 0, but I don't think it's high. Granted, I have the benefit of 15 years of hindsight: I need only three more outbreak-free years. But Sir Martin has the benefit of unforeseen advances in synthetic biology, such as CRISPR/Cas9. We'd need definitions of "casualty" (death, permanent disability, or just infection?), "bioterror" (state-sponsored? apolitical vengeance/spree killing?) and "bioerror" (I assume a mere lack of preparedness doesn't count). But that shouldn't be too hard.
    Casualties will be defined by WHO, CDC, or BPHS, whoever has the highest numbers. Casualties should ideally include "victims requiring hospitalization" and not include indirect deaths caused by the pathogen, although ultimately Long Now will rely on the criteria set by the above organizations for determining casualties to adjudicate this bet.
title: Longbets：到2020年12月，一场生物错误将导致100万人员伤亡
title_en: Longbets: A bioerror will lead to one million casualties by Dec 2020

note:
如果您过去几个月在纽约市的一家餐厅内用餐，您将了解以下常规：您必须在表格上填写联系信息。这样，如果某人的COVID-19测试结果呈阳性，则联系追踪器可以识别在同一家餐厅就餐的其他人，并警告他们可能已被暴露。
  追踪者当然必须知道哪些餐馆（如果有的话）感染了许多顾客。那么，为什么州长Cuomo现在威胁要禁止所有室内用餐，并随着COVID感染和住院人数的增加而施加其他新的限制，而不是只关注表现欠佳的餐馆？
 顺便说一下，餐厅真的是传播的媒介吗？纽约人想知道什么，数据说明了什么？
 答案：我们不知道。是的，你看的没错。这个重要的信息-有关雇用我们成千上万公民的行业的危险性-尚未发布。而不是因为没有人问它：史坦顿岛市议员乔·博雷利，谁日益好像在这个城市的最后一个合理的民选官员之一，已经尝试和失败，以获得它，因为有Rep.选妮科尔·马利奥塔基斯和理事会少数领袖史蒂夫·马特奥（Steve Matteo）。
  “从联系追踪军团获得的数据告诉我们，我们需要重新打开。现在，尽管有成千上万的联系追踪者，但我们没有数据可用于传播目标。 。 。目前还不清楚为什么他们只是恢复到默认锁定，” Borelli说。
 这到底是怎么回事？数据在哪里？餐馆有多危险？
 这里有一些合理的解释。首先，基于奥卡姆（Occam）的剃刀原则，纯属无能的可能性一直是城市政府的驱动力。也许只是没有收集数据而浪费了程序的钱。并非如此，更令人恶心的可能性是我们必须希望的，那就是数据确实存在，但令人不堪重负，以至于对室内用餐的限制似乎是荒谬的。后者将是不可原谅的权力滥用。 
纽约市的餐厅向后，向前，侧身和中间弯曲，以保持25％的开门率。虽然“星期六夜现场直播”是在现场观众面前拍摄的，但史坦顿岛的皮特·戴维森（Pete Davidson）却不戴口罩侮辱自己镇上的持不同政见者，而我们的餐馆-几乎是这座城市的面包和黄油-已被压碎并乞求面包屑。皮特看起来还不错；我想知道为什么我们的餐厅不能满员。
  如果无法在挠痒痒的战斗中笑不出来的无能的小丑可以在室内欢腾，为什么纽约人不能为一个好牛排填满一个房间？
 我们并没有像企业广告客户那样相信我们。随着我们的小型企业遭到破坏，亚马逊正无休止地提高销售量，如果您问为什么，您就是一个坏人。不再需要：如果我们不能去当地的餐馆吃饭，我们需要数据来支持其背后的原理。
  如果城市没有确凿的证据表明餐馆是一个威胁，那就让它们完全营业；如果确实有，请显示出来。十个月来，我们一直被要求信任科学。精细。但是，为了信任科学，我们必须看到科学。目前看来，小企业正被预谋摧毁。
 在最佳情况下，这是过多的警告。在最坏的情况下，这是威权市长和州长以自己的力量喝醉的。没有任何借口隐藏来自纽约的人来说，这数据，更不用说它的民选官员，谁有权利要求它的权利。纽约人受够了。如果餐厅是COVID温床，那么现在我们应该可以证明这一点。
 de Blasio市长和Cuomo市长需要昨天就此问题进行处理。我们不再处于紧急情况之中，而是缓慢的车祸，我们的领导人拒绝转动方向盘。正如上周史泰登岛的抗议活动所显示的那样，纽约人只会忍受这么长时间。市长先生和州长先生请放开。
note_en:
If you’ve dined indoors at a New York City restaurant over the past few months, you know the routine: You must fill in contact information on a form. That way, if someone tests positive for COVID-19, contact tracers can identify others who’ve eaten at the same restaurants and alert them that they may have been exposed.
  Surely the tracers must know which restaurants, if any, have had many of its patrons infected. So why is Gov. Cuomo now threatening to  ban all indoor dining, and put in other new restrictions, as COVID  infections and hospitalizations rise, rather than focus only on eateries with a bad record?
 And, by the way, are restaurants really even a vector of the spread? What, New Yorkers want to know, do the data say?
 The answer: We have no idea. Yes, you read that right. This vital information — about the dangerousness of an industry that employs thousands of our citizens — has not been released. And not because no one’s asked for it: Staten Island City Councilman Joe Borelli, who increasingly seems like one of the last rational elected officials in the city, has tried and failed to obtain it, as have Rep.-elect Nicole Malliotakis and Council Minority Leader Steve Matteo.
  “Data from the contact trace corps was what we were told we needed to reopen. Now that we have no data to target sources of spread, despite having thousands of contact tracers . . . it’s unclear why they are just reverting to their default lockdown,” Borelli says.
 What the hell is going on? Where are the data? How dangerous  are the restaurants?
 There are a few plausible explanations here. First, based on the Occam’s razor principle, is the possibility of pure incompetence, always a driver of city government. Maybe the data simply aren’t being gathered and the program’s money is being wasted. The more nefarious possibility, and one we must hope is not the case, is that the data do exist but are so underwhelming that it would make restrictions on indoor dining seem absurd. The latter would be an inexcusable abuse of power.
 New York City’s restaurants have bent over backward, forward, sideways and in between just to stay open at 25 percent capacity. While “Saturday Night Live” is filmed in front of a live audience, with Staten Island’s Pete Davidson  insulting his own borough’s dissenters without wearing a mask, our restaurants — almost literally the city’s bread and butter — have been crushed and left begging for crumbs. Pete seems to be doing OK; I want to know why our restaurants can’t be full.
  If talentless clowns who couldn’t get a laugh in a tickle fight can gallivant indoors, why can’t New Yorkers fill a room for a good steak?
 We aren’t all in this together as the corporate advertisers would have us believe. Amazon is racking up sales by the boatload as our small businesses are destroyed, and you are a bad person if you ask why. No more: If we can’t go to our local restaurants and enjoy a meal, we need the data to back up the rationale behind that.
  If the city  doesn’t have hard evidence showing restaurants to be a threat, then let them open fully; if it does have it, then show it. For 10 months now we’ve been asked to trust the science. Fine. But in order to trust the science, we have to  see the science. Right now it looks like small businesses are being destroyed on a hunch.
 In the best-case scenario, this is an overabundance of caution. In the worst-case scenario, it is an authoritarian mayor and governor drunk on their own power. There is no excuse for hiding this data from the people of New York, much less its elected officials, who have every right to demand it. New Yorkers are fed up. If restaurants are COVID hotbeds, by now we should be able to prove it.
 Mayor de Blasio and Cuomo need to address this issue head on — yesterday. We are no longer in the middle of an emergency but a slow-motion car crash, and our leaders are refusing to turn the steering wheel. As  protests in Staten Island last week showed, New Yorkers will only put up with this for so long. Put up or open up, Mr. Mayor and Mr. Governor.
title: 为什么纽约的领导人不共享餐馆和Covid-19上的数据？
title_en: Why won’t New York’s leaders share the data on restaurants and Covid-19?

周三，索尼影视娱乐公司（Sony Pictures Entertainment Inc.）宣布，AT＆amp; T和WarnerMedia同意将Crunchyroll出售给Funimation。尽管有几次收购的传言，但并不清楚交易将如何进行或是否会完成。索尼估计这笔交易的收购价格为11.75亿美元。
 “ Crunchyroll团队做出了非凡的工作，不仅发展了Crunchyroll品牌，而且建立了一个热情的动漫迷社区。 Crunchyroll的成功是公司文化和对粉丝的承诺的直接结果。” WarnerMedia的CRO Tony Goncalves在一份声明中说。 “通过与Funimation结合，他们将继续培育全球社区，并为更多人带来更多动漫。我为Crunchyroll团队以及他们在如此短的时间内在数字媒体领域所能取得的成就感到无比自豪。他们已经为这种令人难以置信的艺术形式创建了端到端的全球生态系统。”
  索尼影视娱乐公司董事长兼首席执行官托尼·芬奇夸拉（Tony Vinciquerra）补充说：“我们为将Crunchyroll带入索尼家族而感到自豪。” “通过Funimation以及我们在Aniplex和Sony Music Entertainment Japan的出色合作伙伴，我们对这种全球艺术形式有了深刻的了解，并有能力为世界各地的观众提供出色的内容。我们将与Crunchyroll一起，为粉丝创造最佳体验，并为日本及其他地区的创作者，制作人和发行商提供更多机会。 Funimation已经进行了25多年，我们期待继续利用创造力和技术的力量在这一迅速增长的娱乐领域取得成功。”
 乍一看，索尼从AT＆amp; T收购Crunchyroll可能使他们更容易与Netflix竞争，但此举实际上增加了它们对日本动漫产业的影响力。通过将Crunchyroll的7000万免费会员和300万付费订户添加到其动漫流媒体和制作公司的投资组合中，该公司打算从动漫海外发展中获得回报，在2017年和2018年，动漫市场均增长到该行业超过2.1日元的近一半。万亿美元（约190亿美元）的总收入。
 尽管索尼进入动漫流媒体似乎是突然的，但该公司已经参与了动漫制作数十年。 1995年，日本索尼音乐娱乐公司（SMEJ）成立了Aniplex，这是一家专门管理动漫和音乐作品的子公司。在2005年，Aniplex成立了自己的动画工作室A-1 Pictures，该工作室将为Kaguya-sama：Love is War和Sword Art Online等动画制作动画。
 但在过去五年中，索尼通过收购加强了其国际流媒体服务的业务范围，从2015年开始，法国动漫流媒体服务Wakanim开始了收购。 2018年，该公司收购了澳大利亚动漫发行商Madman Anime及其流媒体服务AnimeLab。一年前，另一家子公司索尼影视公司收购了美国动漫发行商Funimation。然后，在2019年，Aniplex和Sony Pictures Television以Funimation的名义将所有这些流媒体服务合并在一起，作为两家子公司之间的合资企业。
 宣布这一消息的同时，还展示了索尼动漫业务在垂直整合方面的发展，他们揭示了《命运/大订单-绝对恶魔战线：巴比伦》系列的剧集将在索尼国际流媒体服务上享有30天的独家销售权，其他平台。英文配音将有一年的专有权。该动漫系列由Aniplex制作，由CloverWorks（由Aniplex拥有的动画工作室）制作动画，该节目的大部分音乐来自SMEJ艺术家，英语配音由Funimation制作，并且该系列基于Aniplex制作的移动游戏。 
Crunchyroll的数十亿美元的收购价格不是来自联播市场领先的流媒体服务，而是来自它将如何扩展Sony在海外动漫生产中的垂直整合。尽管Aniplex已经在美国Aniplex拥有了北美发行，游戏发行商和销售部门，但Crunchyroll业务的非流媒体部分已经以美国Funimation和Aniplex尚未实现的许多方式进行了扩展。
   该公司于2017年启动了自己的年度大会Crunchyroll Expo，自同年晚些时候成立以来一直是AnimeNYC的主要赞助商。他们还开始联合制作像Kemono Friends和Kino的《美丽世界》这样的新节目，然后在2020年宣布内部制作的节目的“ Crunchyroll Originals”品牌。尽管像Onyx Equinox之类的节目将来自新组建的Crunchyroll工作室，但许多此类节目将与MAPPA和Production I.G等日本动画工作室合作完成。
 在2018年，Crunchyroll还创建了自己的游戏发行公司Crunchyroll Games，以将之前基于流行动漫属性发行的日本手机游戏本地化并分发到英语市场。在2019年，该公司收购了漫画发行商和动漫发行商Viz Media在欧洲的分支机构。索尼现在拥有这一切。
 对于行业和消费者来说，事情从何而来是很难预测的。去年年底，我写了关于流媒体战争期间面临的复杂动画的文章，并预测流媒体服务将更多地参与节目制作，而不仅仅是许可。我的大部分预测是基于这样的想法，即不同的动漫流媒体服务之间的竞争将需要对他们自己的独家节目进行投资，以吸引消费者进行订阅。
 Netflix满足于以每个季节1-3的速度制作自己的独家节目，然后在他们完成国际目录播出后，再许可其他系列节目。如预料的那样，Funimation和Crunchyroll经常参加制作以帮助确保联播流媒体转播权，因此每个赛季都在争夺30至40个新节目的许可。
 但是随着情况的突然消失，除了消费者可能只需要订阅一项或两项服务以同时播放新节目（在短短几年内只需要四到五项服务），消费者就不会在短期内发生太大变化前）。从长远来看，与Sony-Crunchyroll达成的交易有可能减少未来动漫作品的数量和预算。
 但是考虑到此次收购的估值以及过去几年市场的增长，似乎一旦有Funimation和Crunchyroll生产委员会占据一席之地，新的参与者可能会介入以填补空缺。尽管您唯一可能观看联播的地方是Funimation / Crunchyroll。
note_en:
On Wednesday, Sony Pictures Entertainment Inc. announced that AT&T and WarnerMedia agreed to sell Crunchyroll to Funimation. Though there had been rumblings of the acquisition for a few weeks, it wasn’t clear just how the deal would go down or if it would at all. The purchase price for the transaction was estimated by Sony to be $1.175 billion.
 “The Crunchyroll team has done an extraordinary job of not only growing the Crunchyroll brand but also building a passionate community of anime fans. Crunchyroll’s success is a direct result of the company’s culture and commitment to their fans,” said Tony Goncalves, WarnerMedia’s CRO, in a statement. “By combining with Funimation, they will continue to nurture a global community and bring more anime to more people. I’m incredibly proud of the Crunchyroll team and what they have been able to accomplish in the digital media space in such a short period of time. They’ve created an end-to-end global ecosystem for this incredible art form.”
  “We are proud to bring Crunchyroll into the Sony family,” Tony Vinciquerra, Chairman and CEO of Sony Pictures Entertainment, added. “Through Funimation and our terrific partners at Aniplex and Sony Music Entertainment Japan, we have a deep understanding of this global artform and are well-positioned to deliver outstanding content to audiences around the world. Together with Crunchyroll, we will create the best possible experience for fans and greater opportunity for creators, producers and publishers in Japan and elsewhere. Funimation has been doing this for over 25 years and we look forward to continuing to leverage the power of creativity and technology to succeed in this rapidly growing segment of entertainment.”
 At first glance, Sony’s acquisition of Crunchyroll from AT&T might put them in a better position to compete with Netflix, but the move actually grows their influence over Japan’s anime industry. By adding Crunchyroll’s  70 million free members and 3 million paid subscribers to their portfolio of anime streaming and production companies, the company intends to reap the rewards of anime’s growth overseas, which in both 2017 and 2018 grew to nearly half of the industry’s over ¥2.1 trillion (about $19 billion) total revenue.
 Although Sony’s move into anime streaming might seem sudden, the company has been involved in anime production for decades. In 1995, Sony Music Entertainment Japan (SMEJ) established Aniplex, a subsidiary created for managing anime and music productions. In 2005, Aniplex started its own animation studio, A-1 Pictures, which would go on to animate shows like   Kaguya-sama: Love is War and   Sword Art Online.
 But over the last five years, Sony has strengthened its portfolio of international streaming services through acquisitions, starting in 2015 with the French anime streaming service Wakanim. In 2018, the company purchased the Australian anime distributor Madman Anime and its streaming service, AnimeLab. A year earlier, a separate subsidiary, Sony Pictures Television, acquired American anime distributor Funimation. Then, in 2019, Aniplex and Sony Pictures Television consolidated all of these streaming services together under Funimation’s name as a joint venture between the two subsidiaries.
 Along with the announcement came a demonstration of how vertically integrated Sony’s anime business had become, as they revealed that episodes of the series  Fate/Grand Order - Absolute Demonic Battlefront: Babylonia would have a 30 day exclusivity on Sony’s international streaming services before being available on other platforms. The English dub would have a year of exclusivity. The anime series was produced by Aniplex, animated by CloverWorks (an Aniplex owned animation studio,) much of the show’s music was from SMEJ artists, the English dub was produced by Funimation, and the series is based on a mobile game produced by Aniplex.
 Crunchyroll’s billion-dollar acquisition price tag comes not from being the simulcast market leading streaming service, but from how it will expand Sony’s vertical integration in anime production overseas. Although Aniplex already has a North American distribution, game publisher and merchandising arm in Aniplex of America, the non-streaming parts of Crunchyroll’s business have expanded in many ways that Funimation and Aniplex of America had yet to.
   In 2017, the company launched its own yearly convention, Crunchyroll Expo, and has been the major sponsor of AnimeNYC since its inaugural event later that same year. They also began co-producing new shows like  Kemono Friends, and  Kino’s Journey -the Beautiful World-, before in 2020 announcing “Crunchyroll Originals” branding for shows produced in house. Many of these shows would be done in partnership with Japanese animation studios like MAPPA and Production I.G, although shows like   Onyx Equinox will come out of the newly formed Crunchyroll Studios.
 In 2018, Crunchyroll also created its own games publishing label, Crunchyroll Games, to localize and distribute previously released Japanese mobile games based on popular anime properties into English speaking markets. And in 2019, the company purchased the European branch of manga publisher and anime distributor Viz Media. Sony owns it all now.
 Where things go from here for the industry and consumers is difficult to predict. At the end of last year, I wrote about complications anime faced during  the streaming wars, and predicted that streaming services would become more involved in the production of shows not just the licensing. Much of my prediction was based on the idea that the competition between the different anime streaming services would necessitate the investment in their own exclusive shows to entice consumers to subscribe.
 Netflix has been content to produce its own exclusive shows at a rate of one to three every season, then later licensing other series after they finished airing for their international catalog. As predicted, Funimation and Crunchyroll had been vying for licenses to 30-40 new shows each season by often becoming involved in the productions to help secure the  simulcast streaming rights.
 However with that suddenly no longer the case there shouldn’t be much change in the short term aside from consumers potentially only having to subscribe to one or two services to simulcast new shows (compared to the four or five you needed to only a few years ago). In the long term, though, the Sony-Crunchyroll deal has the potential to shrink the number of, and budgets of, future anime productions.
 But given the valuation of the acquisition, and the growth of the market over the last few years, it seems likely new players will step in to fill the spots once occupied by Funimation and Crunchyroll production committees. Although the only place you’ll likely be able to watch their simulcast will be on Funimation/Crunchyroll.
title: 索尼以$ 1.175B收购动漫流媒体服务Crunchyroll
title_en: Sony acquires anime streaming service Crunchyroll for $1.175B

伦敦设计博物馆（Design Museum）安装了由人类细胞产生的牛排，旨在批评肉类工业对动物活细胞的使用不断增加。最终引发了关于生物伦理学和艺术批评陷阱的激烈辩论。
 宾夕法尼亚大学斯图尔特·魏茨曼设计学院的艺术家兼美术副教授Orkan Telhan在去年度过了，他想像着气候变化如何影响未来的食品消费。他与科学家合作创建了一个项目，其中包括3-D打印的煎饼，生物工程面包和转基因鲑鱼。但是，正是他们挑衅性的，开胃的，所谓的“ Ouroboros牛排”（从人类细胞和过期血液中培养出来的肉）的发展挑战了新生的细胞农业产业的可持续发展实践，后者从现有细胞中开发出实验室生产的产品文化。
 自10月份的衔尾蛇牛排（Ouroboros Steak）前往设计博物馆后，围绕该项目动机的激烈网络辩论开始了，这位艺术家收到了数十封威胁性电子邮件和社交媒体帖子，称他为“邪恶”和“纯粹的邪恶”。一些消息要求销毁艺术品。向记者提供电子邮件和推文的特尔汉说，“焦点很快就集中在我们提倡自相残杀的指控上。”
  特尔汉先生补充说：“这是一种错误的解释，它以所有错误的方式被政治化了，因为人们彼此吃饭是一个禁忌话题。”
 “ Ouroboros Steak”以古老的蛇食着自己的尾巴的名字命名，“ Ouroboros Steak”研究但不促进自相残杀，这是对全球对肉类产品需求增长的讽刺意味，科学家警告说，这可能有助于碳排放量和生物多样性减少。设计师希望这一建议引起震惊的听众，可以引起对环境责任和清洁肉行业的审查，尽管大多数公司严重依赖于屠宰过程中收集的胎牛血清，但清洁行业已将自己提升为生产“无杀伤性”食品。孕牛进行细胞培养。
 “我们的项目为解决一个严重问题提供了一种荒谬的解决方案，”生物物理学家安德鲁·皮林（Andrew Pelling）说，他与特尔汗先生和工业设计师格蕾丝·奈特（Grace Knight）合作创建了牛排。 “但是在我们的情况下，您至少要通过带自己的牢房来表示同意。在实验室饲养的肉类世界中，您未经动物同意就从动物体内获取细胞。”
 尽管这个项目引起了争议，但去年一口一口大块的肉在美国各地的博物馆巡回演出毫无问题，即使是在带有银器的盘子上随意摆放。 
策展人米歇尔·米拉尔·费舍尔（Michelle Millar Fisher）说：“我称之为卧铺。”展览的发起人是费城艺术博物馆，然后前往明尼阿波利斯的沃克艺术中心。 “这个项目的核心是非常公平的挑衅。问问自己我们从哪里得到蛋白质很重要。”
 尽管费舍尔女士和特尔汉先生将在即将到来的在线对话中解决对“乌罗伯罗斯牛排”的批评，但尚无计划在展览于2021年3月结束之前从设计博物馆中删除该装置。设计博物馆策展人负责人Priya Khanchandani也为该项目辩护，称其“相当于设计反乌托邦”。
 她补充说：“这是一个有争议的问题，在严重耗尽资源的时代，急需阐明这一点。”
 在过去的几年中，尽管围绕实验室种植的肉的生物伦理学的严肃讨论退居次要位置，但对细胞农业的投资却以惊人的速度增长。市场研究人员估计，到2025年，养殖肉类业务的规模将达到2.14亿美元，到2032年将增长一倍以上，达到5.93亿美元。今年早些时候，美国国家科学基金会的“成长趋同”计划为加州大学戴维斯分校提供了350万美元的细胞拨款。基于实验室的肉类研究。 12月2日，新加坡成为第一个批准使用生物反应器中生长的鸡细胞的政府，并告诉总部位于旧金山的Eat Just公司可以出售其生物工程鸡块。
 非营利性研究机构New Harvest的执行董事Isha Datar说：“如果我们要采用新技术，就需要进行富有成效的批评。” Isha Datar致力于加速细胞农业的突破。 “这项技术有望创造出一种更具可持续性的肉类生产方式，但是我们如何对确保这种情况负责？
 近几十年来，几位艺术家通过采用该领域的方法和机制对生物技术的伦理学提出了质疑。巴西艺术家爱德华多·卡克（Eduardo Kac）于2002年与一个遗传学家团队合作，将白化兔的DNA与发光的水母的DNA拼接在一起，以引起人们对物种特征的转基因杂交可能暗示人类基因组的关注。 2019年，艺术家约旦·伊格尔斯（Jordan Eagles）将血液的放大图像投射到匹兹堡安迪·沃霍尔博物馆的画廊墙上，作为对L.G.B.T.Q.的污名的评论。献血以及那些感染艾滋病毒/艾滋病的人。
 尽管不断有仇恨邮件流入Telhan先生的收件箱，但这位艺术家和他的合作者表示，他们已经收到了来自普通民众的大量询问，这些询问有兴趣购买从自己的细胞中种植肉类的工具包（非出售品） 。佩林博士说，他还收到了几位寻求早日投资“ Ouroboros Steak”或加入加速器计划的风险投资家的询问。但是，暂时还没有将人体细胞制成的肉类推向市场的计划。 
他开玩笑说：“这个项目很挑衅，也许太挑衅了。” “这只是养殖肉兴奋的症状。”
note_en:
The installation of steak grown from human cells at the  Design Museum in London was intended to criticize the meat industry’s rising use of living cells from animals. It ended up triggering a roiling debate about bioethics and the pitfalls of artistic critique.
 Orkan Telhan, an artist and associate professor of fine arts at the Stuart Weitzman School of Design at the University of Pennsylvania, spent the last year imagining how climate change might impact the future of food consumption. He collaborated with scientists to create a project that included 3-D printed pancakes, bioengineered bread and genetically-modified salmon. But it was their provocative, and less appetizing, development of what they call “ Ouroboros Steak,” meat cultivated from human cells and expired blood, that challenged the sustainability practices of the nascent cellular agriculture industry, which develops lab-grown products from existing cell cultures.
 After the Ouroboros Steak traveled to the Design Museum in October, an intense online debate grew over the project’s motivations, and the artist received dozens of threatening emails and social media posts calling him “wicked” and “pure evil.” Some messages have demanded the destruction of the artwork. According to Mr. Telhan, who provided the emails and tweets to a reporter, “the focus quickly became centered on accusations that we were promoting cannibalism.”
  Mr. Telhan added, “It was a misinterpretation that became politicized in all the wrong ways because humans eating each other is a taboo topic.”
 Named after the ancient symbol of a snake eating its own tail, “Ouroboros Steak” examines, but does not promote, auto-cannibalism as a satirical take on the increasing demand for meat products around the world, which scientists  have warned will likely contribute to carbon emissions and reduced biodiversity. The designers hoped that shocking audiences with the suggestion would trigger an examination of environmental responsibility and the clean-meat industry, which has promoted itself as producing “kill-free” food, although most companies heavily rely on fetal bovine serum harvested during the slaughter of pregnant cows for cell cultivation.
 “Our project provides an absurd solution to a serious problem,” said Andrew Pelling, a biophysicist who partnered with Mr. Telhan and the industrial designer Grace Knight to create the steaks. “But in our scenario, you are at least giving consent by taking your own cells. In the world of lab-grown meats, you are taking cells from animals without their consent.”
 As controversial as the project has become, the bite-size chunks of meat toured museums around the United States last year without a problem — even when cheekily displayed on a dish with silverware.
  “I called it a sleeper hit,” said Michelle Millar Fisher, the curator who commissioned the steaks for the exhibition, “Designs for Different Futures,” which began at the  Philadelphia Museum of Art and traveled to the Walker Art Center in Minneapolis. “The provocation at the heart of this project is really fair. It’s important to ask ourselves where we get our proteins.”
 There are no plans to remove the installation from the Design Museum before the exhibition ends in March 2021, although Ms. Fisher and Mr. Telhan will address the criticism of “Ouroboros Steak” in an upcoming online conversation. Priya Khanchandani, head of curatorial at the Design Museum, has also defended the project, calling it “the equivalent of a design dystopia.”
 “It is asking a controversial question that in an epoch of severely depleted resources badly needs to be articulated,” she added.
 Investment in cellular agriculture has increased at a remarkable pace over the last few years, even as serious discussions around the bioethics of lab-grown meat have taken a back seat. Market researchers  estimate that the cultured meat business could reach $214 million by 2025, and more than double to $593 million by 2032. Earlier this year, the National Science Foundation Growing Convergence program awarded the University of California, Davis, a  $3.5 million grant for cell-based, lab-grown meat research. And on Dec. 2, Singapore became the first government to  approve the consumption of chicken cells grown in bioreactors, telling the San Francisco-based company Eat Just that it could sell its bioengineered chicken nuggets.
 “We need productive critique if we are ushering in a new technology,” said Isha Datar, executive director of New Harvest, a nonprofit research institute focused on accelerating breakthroughs in cellular agriculture. “This technology holds the promise to create a more sustainable means of meat production, but how do we hold ourselves accountable to ensuring that happens?”
 In recent decades, several artists have questioned the ethics of biotechnology by adopting the field’s methods and machinery. The Brazilian artist Eduardo Kac worked with a team of geneticists in 2002 to  splice an albino rabbit’s DNA with that of a luminescent jellyfish to call attention to what the transgenic crossing of species’ characteristics might imply for the human genome. In 2019, the artist Jordan Eagles projected  magnified images of blood onto the gallery walls of the Andy Warhol Museum in Pittsburgh as commentary on the stigma associated with L.G.B.T.Q. blood donations and those living with H.I.V./AIDS.
 Despite the ongoing streams of hate mail flowing into Mr. Telhan’s inbox, the artist and his collaborators say they have received a significant number of inquiries from ordinary people interested in purchasing a kit to grow meat from their own cells (it is not for sale). Dr. Pelling said that he has also received inquiries from several venture capitalists looking to invest early in “Ouroboros Steak” or join an accelerator program. For the time being, however, there are no plans to bring meat made from human cells to market.
 “This project has been provocative, maybe too provocative,” he joked. “That’s just a symptom of the excitement around cultured meat.”
title: 人类细胞生长的牛排引起人们的兴趣和愤怒
title_en: Steaks Grown from Human Cells Spark Interest and Outrage

ChloëGrace Moretz（Kickass）在一次秘密任务中扮演一名二战女性飞行员，她必须与一个邪恶的鬼怪角逐，试图在空中阴影中的阴影中空中拆除B-17，这是一部在多伦多国际电影节上首映的恐怖/动作片9月第39届电影节，现在在元旦参加VOD。在预告片的基础上，它看起来像是葛瑞林斯的混搭，在飞机上遇见了蛇，上面还摆满了一些顶级的Vin Diesel风格的动作。
 这部电影的背景设定于1943年，在完成的路上有些坎rock。马克斯·兰迪斯（Max Landis）是英国广播公司德克·肯特利（Dirk Gently）的整体侦探社的创始人，该小说基于道格拉斯·亚当斯（Douglas Adams）小说，是导演约翰·兰迪斯（John Landis）的儿子。 。然后，兰迪斯被八名妇女指控性和情感虐待，并退出了该项目。 （在一条被删除的推文中，导演乔什·特兰克（Josh Trank）与Landis合作拍摄了2012年低预算超级英雄电影《纪事》，他说他相信这一指控，甚至在《纪事》的主要摄影期间甚至禁止Landis参加拍摄。）
 Liang亲自修改了剧本，在赋予女性权力的同时，还保留了恐怖/动作惊悚片的敏感性。这部电影在多伦多的一次乘车仪式上首映，并受到节日参加者的欢迎，甚至赢得了《午夜疯狂》的“人民选择奖”。 （它已经在烂番茄上获得了74％的新鲜评级。）所有这些嗡嗡声促成了与Vertical Entertainment和Redbox Entertainment的发行交易，据称价值约500万美元，这显然是第一部“带座影院”。被好莱坞Deadline如此收购。
    莫雷兹扮演的是女子辅助空军上尉毛德·加勒特（Maude Garrett），他是一名坚韧如钉子的战斗机飞行员，她的秘密任务是提供一个高度机密的神秘包裹。她乘坐的是B-17飞行要塞，被称为“愚人差事”。而且几乎是她的男性同龄人开玩笑和性别歧视的对象。她陷入Sperry或炮塔的职责，但显然她可以胜任这项任务。比日本战机更令人担忧的是邪恶的存在-一颗小鬼怪-威胁要在空中将飞机拆散。
 《云中的影子》将于2021年1月1日首播，该视频将按点播首映。预告片肯定会使这部电影看起来像扎实，高辛烷值的乐趣，甚至还包括对经典《暮光之城》情节的狡猾点头，《噩梦在20,000英尺》 。＆＃34;彼得·德布鲁（Peter Debruge）为《综艺》撰稿，给予了很高的评价，宣称这是一部令人疯狂的娱乐性高空恐怖电影，同时承认这部电影完全具有公信力。＆＃34;它可能只是懒惰的元旦的门票。
note_en:
Chloë Grace Moretz ( Kickass) plays a female WWII pilot on a secret mission who must contend with an evil gremlin attempting to dismantle her B-17 in mid-air in   Shadow in the Cloud, a horror/action film that debuted at the Toronto International's Film Festival in September, and is now coming to VOD on New Year's Day.  Based on the trailer, it looks like a mashup of  Gremlins meets  Snakes on a Plane, with some over-the-top Vin Diesel-style action tossed in for good measure.
 Set in 1943, the film had a somewhat rocky road on the way to completion.  Max Landis—creator of the BBC's   Dirk Gently's Holistic Detective Agency, based on the Douglas Adams novels, and son of Director John Landis—penned the original draft script, and relative newcomer Roseanne Liang was hired to direct. Then Landis  was accused of sexual and emotional abuse by eight women and dropped from the project. (In a  since-deleted tweet, Director Josh Trank, who worked with Landis on the 2012 low budget superhero movie,  Chronicle, said he believed the accusations and had even banned Landis from the set during  Chronicle's principal photography.)
 Liang overhauled the script herself, punching up the female empowerment angle while still maintaining the horror/action thriller sensibility. The film premiered at a Toronto drive-in, and proved hugely popular with festival attendees, even winning the People's Choice Award for Midnight Madness. (It already holds a  74 percent fresh rating on Rotten Tomatoes.) All that buzz led to a distribution deal with Vertical Entertainment and Redbox Entertainment, purportedly worth about $5 million—apparently the first "drive-in theater movie" to be so acquired,  per Deadline Hollywood.
    Moretz plays Women's Auxiliary Air Force Captain Maude Garrett, a tough-as-nails fighter pilot on a secret mission to deliver a mysterious highly classified package. She travels aboard a B-17 Flying Fortress dubbed "The Fool's Errand," and is almost immediately the target of jokes and sexist slurs from her male cohorts. She gets stuck with Sperry, or ball turret, duty, but clearly she's up to that task. More worrying than Japanese fighter planes is an evil presence—a gremlin—that threatens to tear the plane apart in mid-air.
 Shadow in the Cloud premieres on video on demand on January 1, 2021. The trailer certainly makes the film look like solid, high-octane fun, even including a sly nod to the classic  Twilight Zone episode, "Nightmare at 20,000 Feet." Writing for  Variety, Peter Debruge offered high praise, declaring it an "insanely entertaining high-altitude horror movie," while while acknowledging that the film "parts ways with credibility altogether." It might be just the ticket for a lazy New Year's Day.
title: 克洛·莫瑞兹（ChloëMoretz）在《影子在云端》预告片中与飞机上的格蕾林斯战斗
title_en: Chloë Moretz battles gremlins on a plane in Shadow in the Cloud trailer

 现在，随着另一个COVID浪潮席卷美国，Uber Connect可用于在成千上万个新位置发送（和接收）包裹。它本周抵达2400个美国城市，正好赶假期，因为许多地方再次关闭。

 Uber Connect现在位于该应用程序的前面和中央。如果您位于Uber Connect位置，则会看到一个“包裹”信息。 “乘车”旁边的选项和＆＃34; Food＆＃34;纽扣。程序包图标是Uber Connect。
  要发送节日礼物或一盒巧克力曲奇饼，请输入收件人的地址和联系信息。当送货司机到达您的位置时，您在路边碰面，将物品放在行李箱中，以保持非接触式体验。另一方面，如果包裹具有Uber应用程序，则在包裹到达时会通知收件人。您仍然可以在没有Uber的情况下向某人交付东西，这可能需要更多的协调。
 出现问题时，驱动程序会为提供者和接收者提供联系信息，并且可以在Uber应用程序中跟踪所有信息。它类似于您订购汽车时出现的地图。
  对于不太精明的微调，Uber还提供了“接收包裹”服务。向某人请求交货的功能。这可以用作当前请求，但对于隔离的人可能更有用。 
因此，即使这些天您没有订购太多的冰雹，您仍然可以使用Uber应用程序送出礼物和用品。
note_en:
Back in April, Uber was scrambling to provide something for its users who were no longer ordering ride-shares because, well, many of us were stuck at home. The service quickly added a delivery service called  Uber Connect for users to send essential supplies and items to nearby friends and family, but only in a few U.S. cities and other countries.
 Now, as another COVID surge hits the U.S., Uber Connect is available for sending (and receiving) packages in thousands of new locations. It's arriving in 2,400 U.S. cities this week, just in time for the holidays as many places shut down again.
 It'll be available in cities including Atlanta, Charlotte, Charleston, Columbus, Detroit, Fort Myers-Naples, Honolulu, Indianapolis, Jacksonville, Kansas City, Las Vegas, Milwaukee, Minneapolis-St. Paul, Nashville, Richmond, Portland, Salt Lake City, St. Louis, and Tucson.
 Uber Connect is now front and center in the app. If you're in an Uber Connect location, you'll see a "Package" option next to the "Ride" and "Food" buttons. The package icon is Uber Connect.
  For sending out a holiday gift or a box of chocolate chip cookies, you put in your recipient's address and contact info. When a delivery driver arrives at your location, you meet at the curb and put the items in the trunk to keep it a contactless experience. On the other end the recipient is notified when the package arrives if they have the Uber app. You can still deliver something to someone without Uber, it just might take a bit more coordination.
 The driver has contact information for both the giver and receiver if there's an issue, and everything can be tracked on the Uber app. It's similar to the map that comes up when you order a car.
  For a not-so-subtle nudge, Uber also has a "receive a package" feature to request a delivery from someone. This could be used as a present request, but it's probably more helpful for someone quarantining.
 So even if you're  not ordering many ride-hails these days, you can still use the Uber app for dropping off gifts and supplies.
title: Uber表示，它将在4月推出的即日无接触本地包裹递送服务Uber Connect推广到美国的2400多个新城镇。
title_en: Uber says it is rolling out Uber Connect, its same-day, no-contact local package delivery service introduced in April, to 2,400+ new cities and towns in the US

令人震惊的消息是，CentOS项目今天宣布将其Linux发行版转移为基于Red Hat Enterprise Linux的beta（非稳定版）分支，而不是稳定分支。并且他们将在2021年12月31日终止CentOS 8更新。
 CentOS项目现在将发布一个名为“ CentOS Stream”的东西，它是在RHEL的beta分支上构建的Linux发行版，以便贡献者和感兴趣的团体可以解决问题并报告错误，然后将该软件发布到RHEL中。换句话说，在新的RHEL版本发布之前，CentOS将成为RHEL的测试鼠标。
 从历史上看，CentOS Linux发行版始终建立在Red Hat Enterprise Linux的稳定分支上，在两者之间提供二进制兼容性，同时还提供相同的质量和稳定性。 Red Hat在Internet上公开发布了所有软件包的源RPM（SPRMS），而CentOS项目正在做的是获取这些软件包，构建它们，然后以CentOS的品牌重新发行。它还承诺将支持每个CentOS版本长达10年的更新。今天，随着项目的发展，所有这些承诺都被取消了。
 Red Hat在2014年控制了CentOS项目，IBM在2019年控制了Red Hat。绝对可以感谢IBM的背叛。
 这是背叛，因为二十多年来，由于与Red Hat的兼容性以及稳定的10年更新周期，行业中的IT主管和DevOps工程师一直在使用CentOS并向其他人推荐CentOS。人们甚至会推荐在Debian或Ubuntu上使用CentOS，因为与其他受项目影响的项目相比，它提供了“更高的质量和稳定性”。但是今天，CentOS项目成为其自己的营销口号的受害者。
 这是更大的背叛，因为该公告破坏了已经给予的承诺。 DevOps工程师还有1年的时间才能将其基础架构从CentOS完全迁移到另一个替代方案，例如Debian或Ubuntu，而不是最初的承诺在2029年之前得到支持。
 CentOS项目已在其官方页面上更新了其支持期限表，以反映以下更改： 
CentOS在行业中得到了广泛使用，因为它与RHEL不同，它不需要订阅，任何人都可以下载和使用它。因此，您基本上可以获得具有相同软件包和相同更新的免费Red Hat Enterprise Linux。开发人员将充分了解他们开发和打包的CentOS服务器在其本地计算机上旋转，然后与支持其生产企业服务器的RHEL发行版100％兼容。
 所有这些都不幸地结束了。由于没有理智的人敢于在其公司或企业的生产服务器上运行Beta级软件，因此CentOS项目今天才自杀。强烈建议有兴趣的各方从CentOS 8迁移过来，以避免在明年年底之前使用未维护的基础架构。
 当然，任何人都可以从Red Hat中获取SRPM软件包并开始一个类似于CentOS的新项目。但是，为了建立这样一个庞大的项目时间，将需要投资和社区的努力，以使相同的错误不会再次发生。
 从IBM / RedHat方面完全可以理解这一举​​动；他们明确希望IT管理人员只需支付费用并从CentOS迁移到RHEL，以避免停机和从CentOS迁移到其他替代方案所需的巨大努力。从CentOS迁移到RHEL很容易，因为它们使用相同的代码库并提供相同的软件包版本，但是从CentOS迁移到Debian或openSUSE之类的过程则完全不同，因为这些发行版是以不同的方式构建和管理的，因此，需要更多时间和精力来管理迁移过程。
 到目前为止，CentOS项目就是一个例子，说明当企业利益占据社区领导地位时会发生什么。过去，人们经常开玩笑，甚至社区都无法选择每个版本中提供的默认壁纸，但是今天，这些笑话更适合于Red Hat以及他们为CentOS项目所做的大努力。整个DevOps和IT行业。
   每周一次，您可以订阅以接收我们的新闻通讯，其中包含我们在一周内发布的所有内容。不用担心，我们不会发送垃圾邮件。
    哇。 IBM做出的业务决策完全不考虑开源吗？给我上色震惊！ （否）这是非常可悲的。如上所述，CentOS是大多数组织的免费RHEL，没有订阅内容。这是正确和公平的，因为开发团队可以选择和利用CentOS，以确保RHEL在生产中的RHEL能够基于经过CentOS检验和测试的任何内容而工作。现在的动机是使每个人都想使用与RHEL相关的任何东西，以确保他们使用RHEL而不是CentOS。绝对可悲。如果CentOS确实自杀了，我会为之难过，但现实是……阅读更多» 
普林斯顿大学开发的Springdale Linux已经可以替代CentOS。 他曾经将其称为PUIAS Linux，并且比CentOS的存在时间更长。  这不是自杀，这是IBM公司的猪杀人  我完全不同意本文的结论。 CentOS 8还剩下一年的支持……并且CentOS Stream 8将得到支持，直到2024年。Red Hat表示它希望CS8（或C8S？）的性能将比现在的CentOS 8更好或更好……因为它将拥有Red。 帽子开发人员实际上是在进行工作并为此进行质量检查……与过去将CentOS开发人员和Red开发人员分开的非合作墙相反。 虽然这里肯定有一些令人失望的地方，但也有一些美好的事物。 我建议人们拭目以待……阅读更多»
note_en:
In shocking news the CentOS project  announced today that are shifting their Linux distribution to be based on the beta (non-stable) branch of Red Hat Enterprise Linux, rather than the stable branch. And that they are terminating CentOS 8 updates at the 31st of December, 2021.
 The CentOS project will now release something named “CentOS Stream”, which is a Linux distribution built on the beta branch of RHEL so that contributors and interested groups can solve issues and report bugs before the software are shipped in RHEL. In other words, CentOS will become a testing mice for RHEL before new RHEL versions get released.
 Historically, the CentOS Linux distribution was always built on the stable branch of Red Hat Enterprise Linux, providing binary compatibility between the two while also providing the same quality and stability. Red Hat publishes the source RPMs (SPRMS) of all its packages publicly on the Internet, and what the CentOS project was doing is that it was taking these packages, building them, and then shipping them under the CentOS re-branding. It also provided a promise that it will support each CentOS release up to 10 years with updates. Today, all of these promises are canceled as the project takes a new direction.
 Red Hat  took the control over the CentOS project in 2014, and IBM  took the control over Red Hat in 2019. One can definitely thank IBM for this betrayal.
 It is a betrayal, because for more than two decades IT executives and DevOps engineers in the industry kept using and recommending CentOS for others because of the compatibility it offers with Red Hat, and the stable 10-year updates cycle. People would even recommend CentOS over Debian or Ubuntu because it gave “more quality and stability” over the other projects which may be influenced by corporate interest. But today, the CentOS project is a victim of its own marketing slogans.
 It is a larger betrayal because the announcement has destroyed an already-given promise; DevOps engineers have 1 year remaining to completely migrate their infrastructure from CentOS to another alternative, such as Debian or Ubuntu, instead of the original promise of being supported up to 2029.
 The CentOS project have updated their support period table on their  official page to reflect these changes:
    CentOS was widely used in the industry because unlike RHEL, it doesn’t require a subscription and anyone can download and use it. Hence, you basically get a free Red Hat Enterprise Linux with same packages and same updates. Developers would spin a CentOS server on their local machines fully knowing that the software they develop and package on it will be 100% compatible with the RHEL distributions empowering their production corporate servers.
 All of this is sadly ending. The CentOS project has just committed suicide today as no sane person would dare to run beta-grade software on the production servers of their company or enterprise. Interested parties are strongly advised to migrate away from CentOS 8 to avoid using an  unmaintained infrastructure by the end of the next year.
 Of course, anyone can take the SRPMs packages from Red Hat and start a new project similar to CentOS. But in order to establish such project huge time, investment and community effort is going to be needed so that the same mistakes do no get repeated again.
 This move is totally understandable from IBM’s/RedHat’s side; They clearly hope that IT executives would simply pay them and migrate from CentOS to RHEL in order to avoid the downtime and huge efforts needed to migrate from CentOS to other alternatives. Migrating from CentOS to RHEL is easy since they use the same codebase and offer the same package versions, but migrating from CentOS to something like Debian or openSUSE is a totally different process, since these distributions are built and managed in a different way and hence, require more time and effort to manage the migration process.
 The CentOS project is an example, as of this day, on what happens when corporate interest takes the leadership over the community. People used to joke of Canonical in the past and how the community can’t even select the default wallpapers shipped in each version, but today, these jokes are better directed toward Red Hat and the big screw up they did for the CentOS project, and the entire DevOps and IT industry.

CentOS was, as stated, most org’s free RHEL without the subscription stuff. This was valid and fair because devop teams could select and utilize CentOS to make sure RHEL in production worked based on anything stood up and tested with CentOS. Now the incentive is to make EVERYONE wanting to use anything related to RHEL to make sure they use RHEL and not CentOS. Absolutely pathetic. I would feel bad for CentOS if they actually committed suicide but the reality is  …  Read more »
 Springdale Linux, developed at Princeton University, is already a viable alternative to CentOS. It used to he called PUIAS Linux and has been around longer than CentOS.
 This is not a suicide, this is a murder done by corporate pigs from IBM

note_en:
HiFiBerryOS is our version of a minimal Linux distribution optimized for audio playback.The goal isn't to add as much functionality as possible, but to keep it small. Therefore,it is based on Buildroot and it's not possible to use package managers to add moresoftware.
 There is a robust update mechanism that will not overwrite the system, but switch betweenthe current and the new version (they run on different partitions). This is documented in more detail at  doc/updater.md.
         Note that there is no direct support for local music archives (e.g. MP3 files on a NAS) yet. If you have a localmusic collection, you need to use an additional music server to stream music to HiFiBerryOS, e.g.
  You might also configure the included music player daemon, but you have to do this from command line. A simple MPD UI names ympd is running on HTTP port 9000.
  There are several ways to add functionalities to HiFiBerryOS or integrate it into other systems.
  The main backend controller application is called  audiocontrol and offers an API that can be used to start/stop players, switch sources or retrieve metadata.This is often the easiest way if you want to integrate it into other systems, e.g. a automation system.
  Audiocontrol provides a  plugin system that can be used to add more complex integrations.Also have a look at the  "Anatomy of a controller plugin"
  HiFiBerryOS is based on buildroot. This means, you can't easily install additional software from command line. Even if you do, it will be gone after the next update as an update will replace the full filesystem. You need to integrate additonal software via the  Buildroot build system
  The UI also uses a plugin concept. Plugins are called extensions. Have a look at the  Beocreate documentation
      We're looking forward to your contributions. Depending on functionality and code quality, we will decide if a contribution will be included in the base system or will be provided as a user-contributed module that users need to install by themselve.

在昨日的尝试被其猛禽发动机的自动中止擦洗掉之后，SpaceX终于接近为其星际飞船的原型机进行高空测试飞行。该公司已经准备好今天再试一次，并且在暂停倒数计时和重新启动之前的几分钟之内就已经启动了。
 现在，SpaceX直播直播将其目标定为美国东部时间下午5:40的发射时间，如果一切顺利，我们将看到星舰SN8飞到12.5公里（41,000英尺）的高度，并尝试进行创纪录的“着陆翻转机动” ”返回德克萨斯州博卡奇卡基地的路上。为了更详细地了解操作，NASA Spaceflight的发烧友还提供了一个实时供稿，可以从多个角度进行广播，并带有实时评论。
  更新（美国东部标准时间下午6点）：成功发射后，星舰升起并成功操纵到达着陆区的路线。但是，看来航天器的降速速度不足以使其正常降落，并且以令人兴奋的方式爆炸。此刻回想起我们看到的许多猎鹰9着陆尝试，但直到他们成功时才算是成功。
  在现场直播中，SpaceX说测试成功，并指出下一步将继续测试SN9原型。埃隆·马斯克（Elon Musk）在推文中写道：“成功攀登，切换到集水箱和精确的襟翼控制到着陆点！”在后续工作中，他解释了着陆情况，并说着陆燃烧期间燃油总箱压力低，这导致了高着陆速度和大规模爆炸。好消息是，团队获得了“我们需要的所有数据”，而且看来一切都是为了将​​来的测试。
note_en:
SpaceX is finally close to performing a  high-altitude test flight for a prototype of its Starship vehicle, after  yesterday’s attempt was scrubbed by an auto-abort from its Raptor engines. The company is ready to try again today, and came within a couple of minutes of launching earlier before pausing the countdown and restarting.
 Now the SpaceX live stream says it’s targeting a launch time of 5:40 PM ET, and if everything goes well then we will see Starship SN8 fly to an altitude of 12.5 km (41,000 feet) and attempt a record-setting “landing flip maneuver” on its way back to the base in Boca Chica, TX. For a more detailed way to follow the action, the  enthusiasts at NASA Spaceflight also have a live feed that broadcasts from multiple angles with live commentary.
  Update (6 PM ET): After a successful launch, the Starship rose and successfully maneuvered its way to the landing area. However, it appeared that the spacecraft didn’t slow down enough for a proper landing, and it exploded in thrilling fashion. The moment recalls the  many attempts we saw of  Falcon 9 landings that didn’t quite work out until, of course,  they did.
  On the live feed, SpaceX said the test was successful and noted it would be moving on to testing the SN9 prototype next. Elon Musk  tweeted “Successful ascent, switchover to header tanks & precise flap control to landing point!” In a follow-up, he explained the landing, saying that low fuel header tank pressure during the landing burn contributed to the high touchdown velocity and the massive explosion. The good news, is that the team got “all the data we needed” and it appears everything is in order for future tests.
title: SpaceX成功的星际飞船高空飞行以火热爆炸结束
title_en: SpaceX's Successful Starship High-Altitude Flight Ends In a Fiery Explosion

在星期三下午，联邦贸易委员会和一个由48位州检察长组成的联盟对Facebook提出了广泛的反托拉斯指控，以寻求撤销对WhatsApp和Instagram的收购。现在，Facebook在总法律顾问詹妮弗·纽斯特德（Jennifer Newstead）撰写的题为“ FTC和州检察长是修正主义历史的提起诉讼”的广泛帖子中回应了这些指控。
 纽斯特德（Newstead）的主要辩解是，两次收购都为消费者带来了更好的产品-隐含地提及“消费者伤害”标准，这也是司法部对Google提起诉讼的关键。
  Facebook认为：“这些交易旨在为使用它们的人们提供更好的产品，而这些交易无疑做到了。” “联邦贸易委员会和各州一直待命多年，而Facebook投资了数十亿美元和数百万小时，将Instagram和WhatsApp制作成用户今天喜欢的应用程序。”
 Facebook还对分手的追溯性质提出了质疑。两项收购均已有五年以上的历史，并在当时获得了监管机构的批准。
 文章写道：“监管机构正确地允许这些交易向前发展，因为它们没有威胁竞争。”该文章详细介绍了FTC和欧盟委员会对交易的审查。 “这场诉讼有可能引发对美国政府自己的合并审查程序以及并购业务是否能够真正依赖法律程序结果的怀疑和不确定性。”
 追溯审查的问题在法律界是一个开放的话题，但是一些学者认为，监管者重新审视以前的清算交易是有益的。
 该帖子还直接解决了两个诉讼的中心指控，即Facebook将其平台中潜在的竞争产品拒之门外，使亟需用户基础的竞争对手望而却步。对于Vine来说，这种行为尤为明显。Vine在被Twitter收购后立即被Facebook的社交API功能锁定，据报道是在马克·扎克伯格本人的指导下进行的。 
Newstead在帖子中声称，这种API限制是平台的标准做法。 文章写道：“平台允许其他开发人员访问，而许多平台根本不提供访问权限，它们通常禁止重复核心功能。” “ LinkedIn，《纽约时报》，Pinterest和Uber等都具有类似的政策。”  扎克伯格在给《纽约时报》科技记者迈克·艾萨克（Mike Isaac）在Twitter上分享给员工的帖子中说：“总的来说，我们不同意政府的指控，我们计划在法庭上与之抗争。” “现实是我们在所做的每件事中都与许多其他服务竞争，并且我们公平竞争。”  您可以在以撒的推文中了解扎克伯格发给Facebook员工的更多信息：  美国东部时间12月9日晚上7:45：将扎克伯格的帖子添加到Facebook工作人员的推文中。
note_en:
On Wednesday afternoon, the Federal Trade Commission and a coalition of 48 state attorneys general  filed broad antitrust charges against Facebook, seeking a reversal of its acquisitions of WhatsApp and Instagram. Now, Facebook has responded to those charges in an extensive post titled  “Lawsuits Filed by the FTC and the State Attorneys General Are Revisionist History,” written by general counsel Jennifer Newstead.
 Newstead’s primary defense is that both acquisitions resulted in better products for consumers — an implicit reference to  the “consumer harm” standard that has also been central to the Department of Justice’s case against Google.
  “These transactions were intended to provide better products for the people who use them, and they unquestionably did,” Facebook argues. “The FTC and states stood by for years while Facebook invested billions of dollars and millions of hours to make Instagram and WhatsApp into the apps that users enjoy today.”
 Facebook also takes issue with the retroactive nature of the breakup. Both acquisitions are more than five years old, and were approved by regulatory agencies at the time.
 “Regulators correctly allowed these deals to move forward because they did not threaten competition,” the post reads, detailing the FTC and European Commission reviews of the deals. “This lawsuit risks sowing doubt and uncertainty about the US government’s own merger review process and whether acquiring businesses can actually rely on the outcomes of the legal process.”
 The question of retroactive review is an open topic in legal circles, but  some scholars have argued it is beneficial for regulators to revisit previously cleared transactions.
 The post also directly addresses one if the central allegations of both lawsuits — that Facebook locks out potentially competitive products from its platform, starving competitors of a much-needed user base. This behavior was particularly stark in the case of Vine, which was locked out of Facebook’s social API functions as soon as it was acquired by Twitter, reportedly  at the direction of Mark Zuckerberg himself.
 In the post, Newstead claims this kind of API restriction is standard practice for platforms. “Where platforms give access to other developers — and many do not provide access at all — they usually prohibit duplication of core functions,” the post reads. “LinkedIn, The New York Times, Pinterest and Uber, to name a few, all have similar policies.”
 “Overall, we disagree with the government’s allegations and we plan to fight this in court,” Zuckerberg said in a post to employees shared by  New York Times tech reporter Mike Isaac  on Twitter. “The reality is that we compete with many other services in everything we do, and we compete fairly.”
 You can read more of Zuckerberg’s message to Facebook staffers in Isaac’s tweet:
